claims.claim: Excited about zero-knowledge proofs / zkEVMs as the key maturing primitive
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about Ethereum L2 scaling / rollups; cheap abundant blockspace as foundational precondition for apps
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about account abstraction (ERC-4337) / smart accounts for wallet UX and onboarding
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about decentralized social / consumer protocols (Farcaster, Lens) where users own the social graph
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Concerned about US regulatory environment / regulation-by-enforcement (SEC suits, builders pushed offshore, overhang)
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Concerned about centralized intermediaries / counterparty risk; minimize trust in custodians, favor self-custody
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about ETH as the settlement-layer, fee-burning/yield-bearing asset
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Concerned about tokens named in June 2023 SEC complaints (SOL, MATIC, ADA) facing regulatory overhang / delisting risk
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Risk-on, long-horizon build-through-the-bear / deploy-through-the-cycle posture with accelerating fundamental development
claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Crypto × AI and content provenance / anti-deepfake authenticity (ZK provenance trails, attestation graphs) became a major recurring new theme not anticipated in the prediction.
Privacy elevated to a primary thrust in its own right — Aleo private blockchain/Leo, privacy-preserving/decentralized ID, 'privacy is coming to crypto.'

Strong new push to make Bitcoin programmable via ZK/SNARKs ('bring zero knowledge back home to bitcoin,' a VERIFY_SNARK opcode, Bitcoin's long-term security model) — notable given the Ethereum-centric prediction.
Active engagement with proof-of-personhood / Worldcoin / World ID and decentralized identity as a prerequisite for adoption.
Heavy emphasis on smart-contract security and formal/symbolic verification tooling (Halmos, ZK-circuit bug risk, Trail of Bits, SNARK security).
His team shipped Lasso/Jolt SNARK research — producing ZK infrastructure directly, not merely investing in it.
Chain/VM pluralism and advocacy for app-specific chains (Move, Rust, Cosmos/Osmosis), a more chain-agnostic stance that slightly tempers the ETH/L2-maximalist framing.
Explicit conceptual/terminology shift from 'data availability' to 'data publishing'/'data storage.'
notes: All four sector-excitement theses are strongly confirmed by sustained activity: ZK/SNARKs (Lasso/Jolt, ZK circuits, Aleo), scaling/blockspace ('all compute constrained,' onchain games needing L2, data-publishing), account abstraction (smart-account thread, 2024 UX big-idea), and decentralized social/consumer protocols (Nostr critique favoring a unified decentralized computer, Farcaster engagement, Story Protocol, app-layer funding). The build-through-the-bear risk-on posture is the clearest confirmation — relentless infra/research output and explicit optimism about the pace of new projects. Both regulatory concerns (sector-level and the SOL/MATIC/ADA token call) are simply not addressed in the window — absent, not reversed; he stayed heads-down on building rather than commenting on SEC enforcement. ETH-the-asset is marked absent: he affirms the Ethereum/EVM/Solidity ecosystem's network effects but never expresses an asset-level view, and is notably pluralistic about competing VMs/chains. Claim 6 (centralized-intermediary/counterparty concern) is the weakest 'confirmed' — supported via explicit self-custody/'non-custodial fallacy' advocacy and pervasive decentralization-over-centralization framing, though the specific FTX/exchange-concentration/stablecoin systemic-risk framing never appears. No claim is contradicted.
